question: Over $1.2B committed to the MegaETH public sale?

evidences.title: MegaETH's Token Auction Surpasses $1.4 Billion, Boosting ...
evidences.content: The recent token auction for MegaETH has proven to be a significant event in the cryptocurrency market. This Ethereum layer 2 project, which has backing from prominent figures like Vitalik Buterin and Joe Lubin, concluded with total commitments surpassing $1.4 billion. The interest was so overwhelming that the initial coin offering was oversubscribed by 27.8 times its $50 million raise cap, leading to a hypothetical fully diluted valuation of approximately $27.8 billion for the MEGA token. evidences.title: MegaETH Sale 27.8x Oversubscribed with $1.39B in Commitments
MegaETH's public token sale oversubscribed by 27.8x as auction ...
evidences.content: Get started The BlockThe Block # MegaETH’s public token sale oversubscribed by 27.8x as auction officially closes 1 min read MegaETH’s oversubscribed public token sale officially closed on Thursday, with the project raising millions at a "hypothetical" fully-diluted valuation above $27.8 billion. Although the sale, announced last week and structured as an English auction, was capped at around $50 million, MegaETH allowed participants to place bids even after the token rose to a capped clearing price of $0.0999. In total, MegaETH saw investors commit over $1.39 billion in capital at the maximum token price. This means the auction was 27.8x oversubscribed, according to the project's website. [...] The sale, scheduled to run for 72 hours, sold out at its minimum guaranteed token price of $0.0001 at a baseline $1 million valuation within minutes on Monday. MegaETH's English auction also featured a "bimodal allocation process" prioritizing "key contributors" to the ecosystem and ensuring that at least 5,000 participants will receive the baseline allocation starting at $2,650. Investors could place bids ranging between the minimum of $2,650 and the maximum $186,282 per individual, eve...

However, investors are chomping at the bit to try again after committing almost $1.4 billion to MegaETH’s ICO this week. Google Cars speed round a racing track.") #### What to know: Ethereum layer-2 project MegaETH has pulled in $450 million during a token sale that drew nearly nine times its fundraising target within hours of going live. A weighted allocation system factoring in prior community engagement and lock-up commitments will determine final token distributions once the auction closes in two days. evidences.title: Ambition Outpaces Execution in MegaETH's $1B Fundraising ...
evidences.content: - The debacle underscores urgent need for robust infrastructure in blockchain fundraising, where innovation often outpaces execution. MegaETH's Ambitious $1 Billion Fundraising Effort Collapses Amid Technical Failures MegaETH, an EthereumETH-- layer-2 protocol touting real-time transaction speeds, abruptly canceled its $1 billion token sale after a cascade of technical errors overwhelmed its pre-deposit system, leaving investors and industry observers reeling. The project, which had already seen its initial token auction sell out in minutes, faced a catastrophic breakdown during a subsequent funding phase, exposing vulnerabilities in its operational readiness. [...] "The $250M cap is filled by people who were spamming refresh on the Pre-Deposit Website and were able to catch the random opening time," the protocol admitted in a post-mortem statement on X according to reports. Despite freezing deposits at $500 million, MegaETH scrapped all plans to expand the raise to $1 billion, citing a lack of confidence in its ability to manage the process securely. The team acknowledged no assets were lost but conceded the failure reflected poor execution: "At no point were assets at risk, bu...

evidences.content: Advertisement # MegaETH ICO Crosses $1 Billion in Commitments MegaETH ICO Crosses $1 Billion in Commitments MegaETH, a highly anticipated ‘real-time’ Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain, is set to wrap up its initial coin offering (ICO) at 9 am EST on Oct. 30, and despite the raise’s $50 million cap, more than $1 billion is competing for a MEGA token allocation. According to a community-run dashboard, nearly 38,000 users have contributed to the sale with an average bid of around $25,000. the-defiant The ICO is being conducted via Sonar and is MegaETH’s third community-led raise, following an Echo round in 2024 and a soulbound NFT collection, dubbed the Fluffles, that dropped in February. evidences.title: MegaETH token sale oversubscribed by 8.9x with $450M committed
evidences.content: Get started CointelegraphCointelegraph # MegaETH token sale oversubscribed by 8.9x with $450M committed 2 min read ETHUSDT Ethereum layer-2 network MegaETH’s initial coin offering reportedly “sold out” within minutes and is now oversubscribed by about $400 million, as users scramble to get an allocation of its MEGA token. The Ethereum layer-2 network opened the auction for its MEGA token on Monday with a raise cap of just under $50 million, but the auction has since seen more than $450 million in commitments in just a matter of hours. In total, the initial coin offering (ICO) will allocate 5% of the total 10 billion token supply, with a maximum bid amount of $186,282 and a minimum bid of $2,650, offering the option to select a one-year lock-up for a 10% discount. [...] “In the end, MegaETH is getting so much hype because it promises something everyone in crypto wants: a blockchain that’s as quick and smooth as a regular app, but still connected to Ethereum’s trusted network,” Brian Q said. “If the team delivers on its goals, it could become one of the most useful Ethereum extensions yet. But like all early-stage crypto projects, it’s still experimental — so traders and investors...

Soon after the raise announcement, Hyperliquid launched its pre-market perpetual derivatives market for MEGA, placing a more tangible valuation on the project and likely drumming up excitement amongst potential investors. [...] The MEGA pre-market quickly surged to a $6 billion valuation, and currently trades at a $5.2 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV). If the current pre-market price accurately reflects the MEGA launch price, Sonar ICO participants would immediately be up 5x on their investments, assuming the sale closes at the maximum valuation of $999 million. At a $5.2 billion valuation, those who participated in the initial MegaETH Echo round would be up 23x on their investments, and those who minted the soulbound MegaETH NFT collection, known as the Fluffles, would be up almost 10x on their allocations, as the Fluffles are entitled to 2.5% of th...

The "unusual" move comes ahead of MegaETH's mainnet and token launch, expected later this year. The terms of the deal — including total size, financing details, and valuation — remain undisclosed. evidences.title: MegaETH Scraps $1B Fundraise as Technical Failures Block Users
evidences.content: Sonar's rate limit then proved insufficient for the traffic volume. KYC checks failed for more than 20 minutes while the team identified and resolved the bottleneck. When the sale finally opened at a random time intended to ensure fairness, users filled the original $250 million cap within moments. MegaETH attempted to raise the cap to $1 billion at the next hour. But the upgrade executed early because any party can process a multisig transaction once signatures are collected. The sudden change created confusion as some users saw the new window while others did not. The team tried limiting the raise to $400 million, then $500 million, but the cap was reached before adjustments could take effect. With KYC bugs still blocking participants, MegaETH canceled the $1 billion extension. [...] ## What Happened: Fundraise Collapses According to AltcoinBuzz, the pre-deposit event encountered multiple technical failures that forced MegaETH to scrap its $1 billion target. The platform's infrastructure relied on a pre-deposit website that sent user information to Sonar, a service that verifies KYC details, while a pre-deposit contract managed sale timing and caps through a Safe multisig wall...

evidences.title: MegaETH Raises $27.8 Billion in Token Sale - ForkLog
evidences.content: forklog logo forklog logo forklog logo forklog logo forklog hub forklog hub MegaETH Raises $27.8 Billion in Token Sale # MegaETH Raises $27.8 Billion in Token Sale MegaETH's ICO concluded, raising $27.8 billion with oversubscription over 27x. On October 30, the ICO of the second-layer solution MegaETH concluded, raising a total of $27.8 billion. The oversubscription exceeded 27 times. image image MegaETH allowed participants to place bids even after the token reached a maximum clearing price of $0.0999. In total, investors contributed over $1.39 billion at the maximum threshold. The minimum bid amount ranged from $2,650 to $186,282 per user. According to the website, more than 50,000 participants submitted applications. [...] The project team announced that it would begin automatically refunding ICO participants whose bids were below the $0.0999 rate. Next phase: Allocations and Refunds If you bid under $0.0999 → Automatic refunds start today If you bid at $0.0999 → You are being reviewed for allocation. → Review goes until Nov 5th, where allocations will be announced and refunds for unallocated users begin processing. pic.twitter.com/QsF7kfTAIa — MegaETH (@megaeth) October 30,...

evidences.title: MegaETH Opens $1M-to-$999M Auction, Letting Investors Decide ...
evidences.content: Participants can commit up to $186,282, while at least 5,000 users are guaranteed a baseline allocation starting at $2,650. The team said the auction will prioritize long-term supporters, including active community members and early contributors. MegaETH confirmed the sale uses a “bimodal allocation” system to balance fairness and commitment. It combines wide token distribution with deeper allocations for core supporters. If oversubscribed, the team will favor those showing on-chain participation or community engagement. Echo, founded by Cobie, previously hosted MegaETH’s private round, which attracted over 3,000 participants. That event brought in more than $10 million within minutes. This new round follows that same participatory model but opens access to the broader crypto public. [...] MegaETH’s launch through Echo’s Sonar is scheduled for October 27, opening what the team describes as the final step before mainnet. Participants are required to register ahead of time to confirm eligibility. The post MegaETH Opens $1M-to-$999M Auction, Letting Investors Decide Its Worth appeared first on Blockonomi. evidences.title: MegaETH Public Sale Launches on Sonar with Limited Access
evidences.content:  Futures menu tag  Spot  Onchain  Earn  More  menu logo Rewards Hub DownloadEnglish # MegaETH Public Sale Launches on Sonar with Limited Access Share MegaETH is set to launch its public sale on the Sonar platform at 21:00 UTC+8 on October 27. The sale will offer 500 million tokens out of a total supply of 10 billion, with payments accepted in USDT. Bids can range from $2,650 to $186,282, starting at a price of $0.0001 and capped at $0.0999. An analysis by a trader on X highlights that the MegaETH sale is highly selective, with only about 1 in 50 applicants expected to receive an allocation. This approach mirrors previous Echo projects, which have been known for high returns but limited access, suggesting that profits may be reserved for a small group of participants. evidences.content: MEGA PUBLIC SALE USER MANUAL. 10,000,000,000 tokens. Token supply reserved for this public sale. 500,000,000 tokens (5% of total supply). English Auction with a ceiling price. $0.0001 ($1 million FDV). $0.0999 ($999 million FDV).

evidences.title: MegaETH | Public Sale
evidences.content: MEGA PUBLIC SALE USER MANUAL. 10,000,000,000 tokens. Token supply reserved for this public sale. 500,000,000 tokens (5% of total supply). English Auction with a ceiling price. $0.0001 ($1 million FDV). $0.0999 ($999 million FDV). U.S. accredited investors have a mandatory 1-year lock-up with a 10% discount. The lock-up is optional for non-U.S. participants. By clicking "Accept", you agree to the storing of cookies on your device to enhance site navigation, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts. View our Cookies Policy for more information. Identity verification via Sonar is required for anyone who intends to participate in the potential offering. Information such as social media accounts, GitHub account and additional wallets is optional but may increase your chance of receiving allocation in the case of oversubscription. Profile locked when the sale ended. To start, please link ONE wallet you want to use to place bids in the potential sale on Sonar. Using a fresh wallet does not hurt your eligibility to participate. On this website, connect the same wallet linked on Sonar.
